surface_forms: Okinawa was ruled by a king
Okinawa was a territory of Satsuma clan
Okinawa became one of Japan's prefectures
people in Okinawa had to pay the consumption tax
Japanese in Peru are from Okinawa
Japanese in Brazil are from Okinawa
Japanese in Argentina are from Okinawa
description_wiki: Okinawa Prefecture (Japanese: 沖縄県, Hepburn: Okinawa-ken; pronounced [o.kʲi.na.wa(ꜜ.keɴ)]; Okinawan: ウチナーチン, romanized: Uchinaachin) is the southernmost and westernmost prefecture of Japan.

description: reform restoring the imperial rule in Japan in the last third of the 19th century
description_wiki: The Meiji Restoration (明治維新, Meiji Ishin; Japanese pronunciation: [mei.(d)ʑi iꜜ.ɕiɴ, meː-]), referred to at the time as the Honorable Restoration (御維新／御一新, Goi(s)shin), also known as the Meiji Renovation, Revolution, Regeneration, Reform, or Renewal, was a political event that restored imperial rule to Japan in 1868 under Emperor Meiji and led to the westernisation of Japan.

surface_forms: China will make the Southern lord a king
through Okinawa they traded with China
description_wiki: China, officially the People's Republic of China (PRC), is a country in East Asia.
Malaya or Malayan may refer to:
Thailand, officially the Kingdom of Thailand, and formerly known as Siam until 1939, is a country located in Mainland Southeast Asia.

description: samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156
description_wiki: Minamoto no Tametomo (源 為朝; Japanese pronunciation: [mʲi.na.mo.to no (|) ta.me.to.mo], 1139 – April 23, 1170), also known as Chinzei Hachirō Tametomo (鎮西 八郎 為朝), was a Japanese samurai who fought in the Hōgen Rebellion of 1156.

surface_forms: The ancestor of the present 'Sho' family
the present family is the third one

description_wiki: The Tokugawa shogunate, also known as the Edo shogunate, was the military government of Japan during the Edo period from 1603 to 1868.

surface_forms: Satsuma clan could not trade with foreign countries
they took all the good merchandise back to Satsuma
people in Okinawa had to pay tax to Kagoshima as well as Okinawa
description: Japanese historical estate in Satsuma Domain
description_wiki: The Satsuma Domain (薩摩藩, Satsuma-han Ryukyuan: Sachima-han), briefly known as the Kagoshima Domain (鹿児島藩, Kagoshima-han), was a domain (han) of the Tokugawa shogunate of Japan during the Edo period from 1600 to 1871.

description: historical kingdom in parts of present-day Japan
description_wiki: The Ryukyu Kingdom was a kingdom in the Ryukyu Islands from 1429 until 1872, when it was reorganized as the Ryukyu Domain, before being fully abolished in 1879.

Toyotomi Hideyoshi (豊臣 秀吉; Japanese pronunciation: [to.jo.to.mʲi (|) çi.de(ꜜ).jo.ɕi], 16 February 1537 – 18 September 1598), otherwise known as Kinoshita Tōkichirō (木下 藤吉郎) and Hashiba Hideyoshi (羽柴 秀吉), was a Japanese samurai and daimyō (feudal lord) of the late Sengoku and Azuchi-Momoyama periods and regarded as the second "Great Unifier" of Japan.

surface_forms: The system was much different from that in Kagoshima
after Okinawa was occupied by Kagoshima people
It took 1.5 days from Okinawa to Kagoshima
description_wiki: Kagoshima (鹿児島市, Kagoshima-shi; IPA: [ka.ɡo.ɕi.ma, -maꜜ.ɕi, ka.ŋo-]), is the capital city of Kagoshima Prefecture, Japan.

Nagasaki (長崎, Nagasaki; Japanese pronunciation: [na.ɡaꜜ.sa.kʲi, na.ŋa-], lit. 'Long Cape'), officially Nagasaki City (長崎市, Nagasaki-shi; [na.ɡa.sa.kʲi̥ꜜ.ɕi, na.ɡa.saꜜ.kʲi̥.ɕi, na.ŋa-]), is the capital and the largest city of Nagasaki Prefecture on the island of Kyushu in Japan.
Shingon (真言宗, Shingon-shū; "True Word/Mantra School") is one of the major schools of Buddhism in Japan and one of the few surviving Vajrayana lineages in East Asian Buddhism.
The Imperial Valley (Spanish: Valle de Imperial or Valle Imperial) of Southern California lies in Imperial and Riverside counties, with an urban area centered on the city of El Centro.

surface_forms: City of Los Angeles made a regulation
aliases: The town of Our Lady the Queen of the Angels of the Little Portion
description: seat of Los Angeles County, and largest city in California, United States
description_wiki: Los Angeles (often referred to by its initials, LA) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of California, and the commercial, financial, and cultural center of Southern California.

description: county seat of Fresno County, California, United States
description_wiki: Fresno ( ; Spanish for 'ash tree') is a city in the San Joaquin Valley of California, United States.

description: chain of supermarkets based in Boston
description_wiki: Star Market is a New England chain of supermarkets based in Greater Boston.

surface_forms: Did you stop off at Hawaii?
description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Hawaii (  hə-WY-ee; Hawaiian: Hawaiʻi [həˈvɐjʔi, həˈwɐjʔi]) is an island state of the United States, in the Pacific Ocean about 2,000 miles (3,200 km) southwest of the U.S. mainland.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

surface_forms: I got on board the ship from Kobe
I took a ship from Naha to Kobe
description: capital city of Hyogo Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Kobe ( KOH-bay; Japanese: 神戸, romanized: Kōbe, pronounced [koꜜː.be] ), officially Kobe City (神戸市, Kōbe-shi; [koː.beꜜ.ɕi]), is the capital city of Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan.

surface_forms: I took a ship from Naha to Kobe
description: capital city of Okinawa prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Naha (Japanese: 那覇市, Hepburn: Naha-shi; Japanese: [naꜜ.ha, na.haꜜ.ɕi]; Okinawan: Naafa or Nafa) is the capital city of Okinawa Prefecture, the southernmost prefecture of Japan.

description: major river in the western United States and Mexico
description_wiki: The Colorado River (Spanish: Río Colorado) is one of the principal rivers (along with the Rio Grande) in the Southwestern United States and in northern Mexico.
Argentina, officially the Argentine Republic, is a country located in the southern cone of South America and with a claimed portion of Antarctica.
Brazil, officially the Federative Republic of Brazil, is the largest country in South America.
Peru, officially the Republic of Peru, is a country in western South America.
